projects.title: GitHub PM Architecture
projects.summary: A full org-level PM setup built from scratch — YAML issue templates, three Projects v2 boards with custom fields, and GitHub Actions automation for date tracking. Prototyped in a personal demo space before proposing to a real org.

detail.intro: A full org-level GitHub PM setup, built from scratch in a personal demo space before proposing it to a real organization. The goal was to replace a scattered collection of repo-level projects with a coherent, cross-repo architecture using only native GitHub tooling.

sections.heading: What was built
items: A YAML issue template suite covering Epics, User Stories, Tasks, Bug Reports, and Feature Requests — each with structured fields and consistent labelling conventions.
Three org-level Projects v2 boards: a Roadmap (Epics on a timeline by milestone), a Team Planning board (Kanban with iteration support), and a Bug Tracker.
Custom fields including Start Date, End Date, and a Size field (XS–XL mapped to Fibonacci values 1/2/3/5/8) serving as the estimation proxy.
A GitHub Action (set-dates.yml) that auto-populates Start Date on transition to In Progress and End Date on transition to Done, using the Projects v2 GraphQL API.

sections.heading: Key decisions
items: Label-based filtering over issue types — GitHub issue types weren't rendering reliably in project filters, so label-based filtering is the more dependable workaround.
Org-level projects only — repo-level projects lack cross-repo visibility. Org-level is the correct architecture for any team working across multiple repositories.
Size as estimation proxy — avoids introducing a second field and keeps the estimation process lightweight.

detail.callout: Prototyping in a personal demo space before proposing org changes is how you manage stakeholder risk. Nobody likes a surprise restructure.

projects.title: Project Hub Docs Site
projects.summary: A Jekyll + Just the Docs documentation site covering PM workflows, game design references, and onboarding. Auto-deployed on every push via GitHub Actions. Built entirely with native GitHub tooling.

detail.intro: A Jekyll-based documentation site using the Just the Docs theme, covering PM workflows, game design references, and onboarding material. Deployed automatically on every push to main via GitHub Actions.

sections.heading: Tech stack
items: Jekyll with the Just the Docs v0.10.0 theme via remote_theme
GitHub Actions workflow for automatic build and deployment
Structured navigation with section index files and permalink conventions
Hosted on GitHub Pages (free tier)

sections.heading: Site structure
items: GitHub PM — issue templates, project architecture, workflow automation and GraphQL queries
Game Design — GDD template reference and structure documentation
Onboarding — placeholder, to be built out
Roadmap — placeholder, to be built out

detail.callout: Built entirely with native GitHub tooling. No third-party hosting, no paid CI/CD service, no CMS — just a repo, a theme, and a workflow file.
